content.full_text:
TO: [ ] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[ ] LYNN        [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH        [ ] BOB S.
[ ] HELEN       [ ] OTHER

FROM: J. Pritchard
DATE: 12/3/83

[X] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: Gov. Code § 86109 and 2 Cal. Adm. Code § 18620

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [X]

CALLER: Sunny Jung, Auditor
REPRESENTING: Franchise Tax Board
PHONE NUMBER:

QUESTION: Are disbursements made by retained entity to IRS, FTB, Social Security, etc., for taxes and employee benefits, reportable payments by lobbyist employer?

ANSWER: Payments by employers for the employer's share of payroll taxes, employers share of Social Security, etc., are required to be reported as "overhead." However, the amount reported should not include any portion which represents employee income tax withholding or the employee's share of Social Security, etc., because these payments are required to be included in the amount reported as "gross salary" to employee.
